title: maybeReuseLastMonthsGoals_ tries to openByUrl('') when Last Month Tracker config missing
description: signupReuse.js:350-369: when trackerReference is empty, code sets priorResult={ok:false,error:'no-tracker-ref'} then falls through into 'if (!priorResult.ok) { try { ... openByUrl('') } }'. The early no-tracker-ref error is overwritten with open-failed. The control flow should early-exit (return or jump straight to the missing-prior email path) when the config value is absent rather than attempting to open an empty URL.
acceptance_criteria: 1. When Config has no 'Last Month Tracker' row, maybeReuseLastMonthsGoals_ logs the reason, sends the no-reuse email, and returns formResponses without any SpreadsheetApp.open call. 2. test_signup_reuse.js still passes.

title: onFormSubmitLocked_ misplaces Tracker rows on ManagedSheet path
description: addResponseOnSubmit.js:124 reads dataValues to find first empty row in column A, but dataValues is only assigned in the raw-path else branch (lines 109-112) and the catch branch (115-117). On the ManagedSheet path dataValues is undefined, emptyIdx becomes -1, nextRow = trackerLastRow+1. Worse, line 129 calls trackerMs.appendRow which appends at getLastRow()+1 and ignores the computed nextRow. Net effect: blank-row reuse is silently disabled, and the formula-copy/clear block at lines 135-153 may operate on a different row than the ManagedSheet append wrote to. Fix: unify both paths to compute nextRow from a single helper and either both use setValue at nextRow or both use a ManagedSheet API that respects nextRow.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Both ManagedSheet and raw paths compute nextRow identically (first empty >=row4, or lastRow+1). 2. Formula-copy/clear block always targets the same row that received the F3 Name. 3. test_signup_reuse.js still passes.

title: nag.js broken: NAG_EMAIL missing from RESPONSE_COLUMN_MAP causes resolver to throw
description: sendNagEmail calls resolveResponseColumns_(respData[0]) at nag.js:84, then reads responseColumns.NAG_EMAIL via getResponseValue_ at nag.js:106. RESPONSE_COLUMN_MAP in signupReuse.js does not include NAG_EMAIL, and getResponseValue_ throws 'Missing header mapping for NAG_EMAIL' on any non-canonical key. Daily nag emails will fail with an exception every run. Fix: either add NAG_EMAIL to RESPONSE_COLUMN_MAP (canonical header e.g. 'NAG Email?'), or change getResponseValue_/resolveResponseColumns to tolerate optional keys for non-required reads.
acceptance_criteria: 1. resolveResponseColumns resolves NAG_EMAIL when header present; returns undefined/skips when absent. 2. nag.js reads NAG_EMAIL without throwing when column exists. 3. nag.js treats missing NAG_EMAIL as opted-out (no throw, no break). 4. test_signup_reuse.js still passes.

title: Document NoticeLog sidebar-only contract and add dual logging
description: NoticeLog() is exclusively a sidebar UI mechanism — it enqueues messages to the TO_CLIENT PropertiesService queue and has no Logger.log() call. It is only active when NoticeLogInit() has opened a sidebar (i.e. copyAndInit() and reinitializeSheets()). Calls to NoticeLog() outside that context silently discard the message with no server-side trace. Two changes needed: (1) Add a Logger.log() call inside NoticeLog() so every sidebar message is also captured in the Apps Script execution log. (2) Update the file header comment and JSDoc on NoticeLog(), NoticeLogInit(), and NoticePrompt() to clearly state that these functions are sidebar-only and must not be used in trigger-fired or background functions. Update README.md DESIGN section to document this contract.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: NoticeLog() calls Logger.log() with the same message (stripping HTML tags for readability). AC2: NoticeLog(), NoticeLogInit(), and NoticePrompt() have JSDoc that states sidebar-only context requirement. AC3: README.md DESIGN section documents the NoticeLog contract and when it is and is not available. AC4: Any call sites of NoticeLog() in trigger-fired functions are flagged or replaced with Logger.log().

title: writeReusedValuesToResponseRow makes 7 single-cell setValue round-trips
description: signupReuse.js:168-172 iterates the reused-values map and calls responsesSheet.getRange(row, col).setValue(...) per field — 7 sheet API round-trips per reuse submission. Concurrent submissions amplify cost and quota usage. Replace with a single setValues over the contiguous slice (or a ManagedSheet.updateRow batched write) — the goal columns are mostly contiguous, and a sparse setValues pattern is still one round-trip.
acceptance_criteria: 1. writeReusedValuesToResponseRow makes exactly 1 sheet write call (not 7). 2. All 7 goal fields are written correctly. 3. test_signup_reuse.js still passes.

title: isReuseLastMonthsGoalsChoice fragile: regex on free-text form option
description: signupReuse.js:108 matches /^yes\b/ AND lower.indexOf('last') !== -1 against the participant's PARTICIPATION answer. If the form option is reworded (e.g., 'Yes — copy my previous goals'), reuse silently breaks with no error. Options: (a) match the form option text exactly, (b) read the trigger phrase from a Config row, (c) key off a hidden form item id. Pick one and document it.
acceptance_criteria: 1. When Config has 'Reuse Goals Trigger' row, isReuseLastMonthsGoalsChoice matches that exact phrase (case-insensitive). 2. When Config absent, current regex heuristic still applies. 3. test_signup_reuse.js still passes.

title: getPriorResponse silently falls back to raw sheet when ManagedSheet header check fails
description: signupReuse.js:248-253 catches errors from openExistingManagedSheet/openExistingSheet and proceeds to a raw-sheet fallback when prevMs is null. If the previous tracker is missing canonical headers in RESPONSE_COLUMN_MAP, the fail-fast intent is bypassed and a more permissive raw-sheet code path runs instead. This contradicts the documented fail-fast posture. Decide on one policy: either fail loudly when prior tracker headers don't match, or document and standardize the fallback.
acceptance_criteria: 1. getPriorResponse does not fall back to raw sheet; if ManagedSheet open fails, returns {ok:false, error, message}. 2. test_signup_reuse.js still passes.

title: Two emails sent per single reuse submission
description: On a successful reuse submission the participant receives two emails: copyResponsesToCurrentTracker calls sendResponseSettingsEmail (response_utils.js:64) and maybeReuseLastMonthsGoals_ then calls sendGoalReuseEmail (signupReuse.js:419). Decide which is canonical and remove the other. Recommended: keep sendGoalReuseEmail (includes prefilled URL for adjustments) and stop the redundant copyResponsesToCurrentTracker invocation from the reuse path.
acceptance_criteria: 1. A single reuse submission triggers exactly one email to the participant. 2. test_signup_reuse.js still passes.

title: Two writers race on current Responses row during reuse flow
description: maybeReuseLastMonthsGoals_ calls writeReusedValuesToResponseRow at signupReuse.js:376, then later calls copyResponsesToCurrentTracker(emailAddress) at signupReuse.js:411. The latter (response_utils.js) independently rewrites the same row from the previous tracker using buildSharedHeaderCopyPlan_, which can overwrite the values just written — including non-goal columns sharing header names (Timestamp, Email, etc.). Decide on a single writer and remove the other call. Likely keep writeReusedValuesToResponseRow and stop invoking copyResponsesToCurrentTracker from the reuse path.
acceptance_criteria: 1. maybeReuseLastMonthsGoals_ no longer calls copyResponsesToCurrentTracker. 2. Current Responses row is updated exactly once (by writeReusedValuesToResponseRow). 3. test_signup_reuse.js still passes.

title: Column-hiding logic hides non-empty columns when month has 31 days
description:
## Problem
May 2026 tracker caused column AT to be hidden even though AS was the final Bonus column — hiding a column that contained data.

The hiding logic miscalculates the last active column, potentially hiding columns beyond the intended range.

## Proposed Fix Direction
1. Add a guard: never hide a column if row 3 of that column contains text.
2. Consider replacing the current offset-based logic entirely: hide any column where row 3 is empty, rather than computing an end column from day count.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The hiding logic never hides a column whose row 3 is non-empty.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Open or generate the May 2026 tracker.
2. Observe column AT is hidden even though column AS has a header in row 3.

title: urlShortener: retry never fires on TinyURL 422 alias-conflict — case-sensitive match
description: TinyURL returns HTTP 422 with body {"errors":["Alias is not available."]} when an alias is already taken. The retry guard in shortenUrl checks error.message.includes("alias") (lowercase), but the thrown error contains "Alias" (capital A) from TinyURL's response. JavaScript includes() is case-sensitive so the match fails, the error falls into the rethrow branch, and shortenUrl throws immediately with no retries attempted. All 5 retry attempts (with -1, -2 suffixes) are silently skipped. Confirmed in cloud log 2026-04-01 05:47:11: first and only attempt logged, then caught by caller. Fix: change the includes check to case-insensitive (toLowerCase()).
acceptance_criteria: shortenUrl retries with alias-N suffixes when TinyURL returns 422 alias-conflict; confirmed in cloud log that multiple alias attempts are made before falling back to raw URL

title: copyAndInit: Config sheet not hidden in new spreadsheet
description: copyAndInit() copies the template spreadsheet. The Config sheet (which holds sensitive data like Site Q email) is not hidden in the new spreadsheet. It should be hidden (hideSheet) so end users cannot see it. The fix is to call configSheet.hideSheet() in copyAndInit after initialization.

title: copyAndInit: Activity sheet not cleared in new spreadsheet
description: copyAndInit() copies the template spreadsheet, which has ~494 rows of historical Activity log data. The Activity sheet is not cleared as part of initialization. The new spreadsheet should have only the header row on Activity. The fix is to add a clearContent on Activity rows 2+ in copyAndInit (similar to how Bonus Tracker is cleared at line 270).

title: populateTrackerSheet: add trailing Bonus column after last day of month
description: populateTrackerSheet only adds a Bonus column after Saturday dates. When the last day of the month is not a Saturday, no trailing Bonus column is created. AC says a final Bonus column must exist after the last day regardless of weekday. The fix is to call setBonusColumn once after the while loop ends, using the current column and bonus count.

title: Log urlShortener failures to LogFile from caller
description:
When urlShortener fails to create a short URL, the failure is currently silent — the caller receives a raw URL fallback with no indication of what went wrong. This makes debugging copyAndInit failures from the LogFile impossible.

The fix should be in the caller (CreateNewTracker.js), not inside urlShortener.js, to keep urlShortener portable and loosely coupled. The caller should detect when the returned URL is not a TinyURL (i.e. still a raw Google URL) and append an error entry to the LogFile.

Design intent:
- urlShortener returns the raw URL on failure (current behavior, keep as-is)
- Caller checks the returned value: if it does not start with 'https://tinyurl.com', log a warning entry to the LogFile with the alias attempted and the raw URL returned
- No changes to urlShortener.js interface or internals

title: Implement LogFile infra and automation for UC-5 (LogFile-driven GAS testability)
description:
UC-5: Implement LogFile infrastructure to allow bound GAS test automation.

Goals:
- Create/maintain a Drive logfile URL saved in the  sheet ( row).
- Append structured JSON log entries for menu actions, triggers, and test runs (timestamp, trigger name, payload, sidebar HTML, URLs).
- Ensure logfile creation sets 'anyoneWithLink' read permission and updates .
- Provide a small test harness (GAS + downloadable log) and docs for using logs to automate assertions in .

Notes: logfile may contain Site Q email and URLs; set permissions accordingly and document security constraints.
acceptance_criteria:
1)  and  append JSON entries to Drive log and update  with valid URL.
2) Running the provided test harness downloads the log and verifies expected keys (timestamp, trigger, payload).
3) README updated to document UC-5 automation steps and security notes.
4) bd issue references tests and example usage.
close_reason: Implemented getOrCreateLogFile_, appendToLogFile_ (logFile.js), instrumented copyAndInit(), added log_channel.py helper, updated README with UC-5 automation steps and security notes

title: Verify About menu and version constants via LogFile
description: Using the LogFile test channel, verify that version.js constants are correct and the About menu item renders correctly. Operator: clasp push to dev spreadsheet, open spreadsheet, trigger About menu item. LogFile should capture the modal HTML. Assert: version string '1.1.0', date '2026-03-02', author info present in payload.
close_reason: Human confirmed About modal renders correctly. Version rolled to 2.0.0 (2026-03-31) since issue was written.

title: Integrate LogFile writes into copyAndInit() and sidebar
description: Instrument copyAndInit() to write a log entry via appendToLogFile() capturing: tracker URL, form URL, Site Q name and email read from Config, HTML string passed to the sidebar, confirmation message set on HC form, and any errors encountered. Log entry written after all operations complete (or on error). Depends on LogFile infrastructure and append helper.

title: Python log download and assertion helper for test scripts
description: Add a shared test utility (test/log_channel.py or similar) that: (1) accepts a Drive file URL or ID, (2) downloads the file content via HTTP (no auth required — anyone with link), (3) parses log entries by the === header delimiter, (4) returns a list of dicts {timestamp, trigger, payload} for assertion. Test scripts import this helper and assert on payload keys/values. Depends on LogFile infrastructure being in place so a real file URL can be used in testing.

title: LogFile append helper: structured log entry writer
description: Implement appendToLogFile(fileId, triggerName, payload) where payload is a plain JS object. Writes a log entry as: '=== [ISO timestamp] [triggerName] ===\n' followed by JSON.stringify(payload, null, 2) and a trailing newline. Appends to existing content. Used by all GAS functions that participate in the test channel.

title: LogFile infrastructure: Config check, Drive create, URL persist
description: Implement the LogFile test channel foundation. On any GAS function that participates in logging, call getOrCreateLogFile(): (1) read Config sheet for 'LogFile' variable (Column A), (2) if Column B is empty or missing, create a plain-text Drive file named 'F3Go30-LogFile' with 'anyone with the link' read permissions and write the URL back to Config Column B, (3) return the file ID for use by the log append helper. Drive scope must be present in appsscript.json.

title: [bug] copyAndInit: early return inside try block orphans new spreadsheet silently
description: Three early-return paths in the try block (parent folder not found at line 52, Tracker sheet not found at line 64, no linked form at line 83) call NoticeLog + return without logging the orphaned spreadsheet ID. The catch block at line 129 correctly logs the orphan ID on throw, but these return paths bypass it, leaving users with a mystery orphaned spreadsheet and no cleanup instructions.

title: [bug] copyAndInit: copy() call outside try block gives no user error on failure
description: currentSpreadsheet.copy() at CreateNewTracker.js:43 is outside the try block. If copy() throws (quota exceeded, permissions error), the error propagates without any NoticeLog message — the sidebar shows nothing and the user has no indication of the failure.

title: [bug] onFormSubmit double-fires: simple trigger + installable trigger both active
description: Naming the handler 'onFormSubmit' makes it a GAS simple trigger automatically. setupFormSubmitTrigger() also creates an installable trigger pointing to the same function. Both fire on every form submission, causing logActivity and sort to run twice. The duplicate-name guard (line 76) masks double-insertion but other side effects double-execute.

title: [bug] onFormSubmit: lock timeout silently discards form submission
description: When runWithLock returns false (30s timeout), the event object e is discarded with no log entry. The form response is permanently lost with no way to recover which PAX submitted. Quick win: before returning, add Logger.log('onFormSubmit: lock timeout — event: ' + JSON.stringify(e));

title: [bug] NoticePrompt busy-wait can exhaust 6-minute GAS execution limit
description: NoticePrompt polls in while(!response) with 1-second sleeps and no timeout. If the user takes over 6 minutes or abandons the sidebar, GAS kills the execution mid-copyAndInit, leaving a partially-initialized spreadsheet and orphaned Drive files in an unrecoverable state. Fix: add a max-iteration count (e.g. 120 iterations = 2 min) and return null on timeout; display remaining time in the sidebar.

title: [bug] initMessageQueues clears PropertiesService queues without lock protection
description: initMessageQueues() calls setScriptProperty three times (TO_SERVER, TO_CLIENT, LastClientAction) outside of runWithLock. If a concurrent onFormSubmit trigger is mid-Enqueue when initMessageQueues runs, the queue write is wiped, silently losing the enqueued message. Fix: wrap the three setScriptProperty calls inside runWithLock.

title: [bug] markEmptyCellsAsMinusOne overwrites formula cells that evaluate to empty string
description: The function reads getValues() and writes -1 to any cell where values[j][0] === ''. Formula cells that evaluate to '' (e.g. attendance formulas for future dates) return '' from getValues(), so the function overwrites the formula with the literal value -1. Fix: also read getFormulas() for the range and skip cells where formulas[j][0] !== ''.

title: [bug] onFormSubmitLocked_: missing null check on responsesSheet and destinationSheet
description: getSheetByName('Responses') and getSheetByName('Tracker') (lines 42-43) are used immediately without null checks. A renamed or missing sheet causes an unhandled exception that silently drops the form submission with no recovery path. Quick win: add after line 43 — if (!responsesSheet || !destinationSheet) { Logger.log('onFormSubmit: required sheet not found'); return; }

title: [bug] copyAndInit leaves orphaned Drive files on mid-run failure
description: After currentSpreadsheet.copy() (line 43), no try/catch wraps the remainder of copyAndInit. Any failure (initSheets throws, execution timeout, API error) leaves the copied spreadsheet and linked form in Drive in a broken state — partially initialized, possibly wrong permissions, no triggers — with no recovery information shown to the user. Fix: wrap body in try/catch; on failure log the new spreadsheet ID/URL so the user can delete or manually complete it.

title: [bug] onFormSubmit uses getLastRow() instead of e.range — duplicate PAX race
description: onFormSubmitLocked_ reads responsesSheet.getLastRow() to identify the submitted row, but ignores the event object e entirely. When two submissions arrive nearly simultaneously and both triggers fire, both acquire the lock serially but both read the same getLastRow() — causing one PAX to be processed twice and the other skipped. Fix: use e.range or e.values to identify the exact submitted row. Note: F3Go30-3e8 is about caching/TextFinder performance; this is a data-correctness race.

title: [perf/bug] Per-row API call loop in onFormSubmit holds lock too long
description: In addResponseOnSubmit.js lines 79-82, the empty-row search uses a while loop calling getRange(nextRow,1).getValue() once per row — potentially 50+ round-trips on a large tracker, all while the script lock is held. This starves concurrent submissions. Fix: replace with a single batch read getRange(4,1,lastRow-3,1).getValues() and find the first empty entry in JS.
close_reason: Replaced per-row getValue() while loop with findIndex() on the already-fetched dataValues array — zero extra API calls.

title: [bug] getParents().next() has no hasNext() guard in copyAndInit
description: In CreateNewTracker.js line 48, currentSpreadsheetFile.getParents().next() is called without checking hasNext() first. If the spreadsheet is in My Drive root (no parent folder), the iterator is empty and .next() throws, aborting copyAndInit mid-run with no error shown in the sidebar. Fix: add if (!parents.hasNext()) { NoticeLog('Error: cannot determine folder'); return; } before .next().
close_reason: Added hasNext() guard before .next() on getParents() iterator; returns early with NoticeLog error if spreadsheet is in My Drive root.

title: [bug] Responses sheet write in onFormSubmit occurs outside lock scope
description: In addResponseOnSubmit.js lines 56-58, the Responses sheet is mutated (writing column G's value into column F when F is empty) before the LockService script lock is acquired. Concurrent form submissions can corrupt the Responses sheet. Fix: move the Responses-sheet write inside the lock scope, or carry the fallback value in a local variable only.
close_reason: Removed responsesSheet.getRange(lastRow, 6).setValue() — team value is logged only; form data source no longer mutated. Lock scope was correct; the mutation itself was the problem.

title: [bug] InspireNow() row-count arithmetic unsafe on empty Inspiration sheet
description: In onOpen.js lines 131-141, if the Inspiration sheet has 0 rows, data.length-1 is -1 and Math.random()*-1 produces a negative array index, returning undefined and likely causing a downstream error. (The null check for the sheet object itself is tracked in F3Go30-cnk; this is the row arithmetic.) Fix: add if (data.length < 2) { Logger.log('InspireNow: no inspiration rows'); return; } after fetching data.
close_reason: Added data.length < 2 guard after getDataRange().getValues(); logs and returns early on empty Inspiration sheet.

title: [bug] getFormUrl() not null-checked before FormApp.openByUrl() in copyAndInit
description: In CreateNewTracker.js lines 74-75, getFormUrl() is called on the copied spreadsheet and passed immediately to FormApp.openByUrl(). If the template has no linked form, getFormUrl() returns null and openByUrl(null) throws, aborting copyAndInit mid-run with no sidebar error. Fix: add null guard — if (!formUrl) { NoticeLog('Error: no form linked to template'); return; } — before opening the form.
close_reason: Added null guard for formUrl after getFormUrl(); returns early with NoticeLog error if template has no linked form.

title: [bug] innerHTML XSS risk in NotificationSidebar.html
description: updateTitle(), updateDescription(), and updateLog() (lines 42, 48, 98) assign arbitrary server-supplied strings directly to innerHTML. A PAX name or log message containing HTML event attributes (e.g. ) would execute in the sidebar context. Fix: use textContent for plain-text messages; only use innerHTML for values explicitly constructed as safe HTML (e.g. createHtmlLink output).
close_reason: Fixed updateTitle and updateDescription to use textContent instead of innerHTML. updateLog retains innerHTML as it intentionally receives server-constructed HTML (bold tags, links, textareas).

title: [bug] runWithLock silently swallows lock acquisition failures
description: NotificationSBCode.js:98-108 — When waitLock times out and throws, the error is caught and logged but runWithLock returns normally. Callers Enqueue and Dequeue have no way to detect failure; queue writes and reads are silently dropped.

title: [bug] onFormSubmit sort excludes newly added PAX row (stale row count)
description: addResponseOnSubmit.js:72,121 — trackerLastRow is captured before the new PAX row is inserted. The sort on line 121 uses trackerLastRow - 3 as row count, so the newly inserted row is outside the sort range and never sorted.

title: [bug] onFormSubmit nextRow search starts at row 3, can overwrite date header
description: addResponseOnSubmit.js:89 — var nextRow = 3 begins the empty-row search from the date header row. If Tracker!A3 is ever blank, a PAX F3 name overwrites the date header. Data model specifies PAX data starts at row 4.

title: Fix onFormSubmit response bounds check (< 4 should be < 7)
description: formResponses length guard checks < 4 but code accesses indices 5 and 6. If form has 4-6 fields, indices 5 and 6 are undefined and the code silently misbehaves. Guard must be < 7.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: If form response has fewer than 7 fields, onFormSubmit logs and exits. AC2: Existing behavior unchanged for 7+ field responses.

title: Fix markMinusOne column guard off-by-one (> 1 should be > 0)
description: thresholddayColumnIndex initializes to -2. Guard condition is > 1, so column index of 1 (column A) is incorrectly skipped. Should be > 0. Also no log when column is not found.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: Guard is > 0. AC2: Logger.log emitted when threshold day column is not found.

title: Fix race condition in onFormSubmit with LockService
description: Two simultaneous form submits can both pass the duplicate check before either writes, resulting in duplicate Tracker entries. Wrap onFormSubmit() body in LockService.getScriptLock() to serialize concurrent executions.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: onFormSubmit acquires a script lock before reading Tracker. AC2: If lock cannot be acquired within 30s, function logs and exits. AC3: Existing behavior unchanged for single submissions.

title: Use NameSpace from Config to auto-generate spreadsheet name and URL aliases
description: Currently copyAndInit() prompts the operator to type the spreadsheet name manually, and URL aliases reference a hardcoded F3Go30 string. The spreadsheet name should be auto-generated as YYYY-MM-NameSpace (e.g. 2026-04-F3Waxhaw) using the NameSpace value from Config\!B where Config\!A='NameSpace', combined with the start date entered by the operator. The short URL aliases (trackerAlias, formAlias) should likewise use NameSpace instead of the current fallback. Remove the NoticePrompt for the spreadsheet name — derive it from Config + startDate instead.
design: Format is YYYY-MM-NameSpace. NameSpace read from Config sheet (see F3Go30-6u5). Removes the manual name prompt from copyAndInit().
acceptance_criteria: AC1: Spreadsheet name is auto-generated as YYYY-MM-NameSpace without prompting the operator. AC2: trackerAlias and formAlias use NameSpace in the URL alias string. AC3: If NameSpace is missing from Config, copyAndInit() surfaces a clear error and exits. AC4: README.md DESIGN section documents the naming convention.
close_reason: NameSpace auto-naming implemented; default 'F3 Go30' written to Config on first run if missing

title: Document and use Config sheet for runtime configuration
description: The tracker spreadsheet has a Config sheet. Column A is the variable name, column B is the primary value, column C is a secondary value. Known variables: 'Site Q' (B=name, C=email address), 'NameSpace' (B=value). Code that needs Site Q name/email or NameSpace must read from Config, not hardcode values. Update README.md DESIGN section to document the Config sheet schema. Update any code that currently hardcodes these values to read from Config instead.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: README.md DESIGN section documents the Config sheet schema (column layout and known variable names). AC2: copyAndInit() reads Site Q email from Config\!C where Config\!A='Site Q'. AC3: Any other code using Site Q name/email or NameSpace reads from Config. AC4: If a required Config value is missing, the calling function surfaces a clear error.
notes: Known Config variables: 'Site Q' (B=name, C=email), 'NameSpace' (B=value used to derive spreadsheet name as YYYY-MM-NameSpace and URL aliases). See F3Go30-dgx for naming usage, F3Go30-xf0 for email usage.

title: Send Site Q onboarding email after copyAndInit
description: After copyAndInit() completes successfully, send an email to the Site Q containing: (1) instructions to open the tracker sheet and verify it is correct, (2) instructions to run Initialize Triggers, (3) instructions to open the HC form and verify it is correct, (4) the Slack ready message (from F3Go30-dot) so the Site Q can post it once they have verified everything. The monthly tracker is not considered complete until this email is sent and the operator has confirmed receipt. Site Q email address must be available at copyAndInit() time — likely from the tracker sheet or passed as a parameter.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: copyAndInit() sends an email to the Site Q upon successful completion. AC2: Email contains verification checklist (open tracker, run Initialize Triggers, verify HC form). AC3: Email contains the Slack ready message. AC4: If Site Q email is unavailable, copyAndInit() surfaces an error rather than silently skipping. AC5: Operator can confirm email was received before closing out the monthly setup.
notes: Site Q email address is stored in the Config sheet. Read it from there at copyAndInit() time — no need to prompt the operator.
close_reason: Misscoped: email to Site Q belongs in autoGenerateNextMonthTracker() only (manual operator is already present). autoGenerateNextMonthTracker() already sends this email with slackMsg via buildSlackMessage_(). Email removed from copyAndInit().

title: Generate Slack copy-paste messages after Copy and Initialize
description: After copyAndInit() completes, the sidebar should present: (1) a Slack canvas update block (formatted text with tracker URL, form URL, Site Q info) and (2) a Slack notice/announcement message — both ready to copy/paste. Display in sidebar with a copy button or clearly demarcated block. Delivers UC-1.
close_reason: buildSlackMessage_() extracted to Utilities.js; copyAndInit sidebar shows Slack block; slackMessage in LogFile payload; test asserts content; README UC-1 and payload table updated

title: Add guard: onFormSubmit range size check
description: onFormSubmit range calculation can throw if Tracker has fewer than 4 rows. Add a guard to check range size before calling getRange.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: If Tracker has fewer than 4 rows, onFormSubmit exits without throwing a range error. AC2: If Tracker has 4 or more rows, existing behavior is unchanged. AC3: Early exit logs a message indicating why it returned.

title: Fix macros.js / onOpen.js overlap
description: Consolidate trigger setup into one path; remove or clearly disable startNewMonth(). startNewMonth() currently calls initSheets() with no arguments, causing a runtime error. macros.js has legacy entry points that duplicate trigger setup logic in onOpen.js and addResponseOnSubmit.js.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: startNewMonth() is removed or clearly disabled — cannot be accidentally invoked. AC2: Trigger setup logic has one canonical location; any duplicate in macros.js is removed. AC3: All other macros.js functions that are still needed remain intact and callable from the menu.

title: Verify version.js constants and About menu in dev spreadsheet
description: Version.js was added with an About menu item but has not been deployed or tested. Verify constants and menu rendering in a dev spreadsheet before deploying via clasp push.
notes: Code is complete — version.js constants exist, onOpen.js wires About menu item and showAbout(). Requires manual: clasp push to dev spreadsheet, open spreadsheet, verify F3 Go30 Menu > About renders the modal with version 1.1.0 (2026-03-02) and author info.
close_reason: Human confirmed About modal renders correctly. Version rolled to 2.0.0 (2026-03-31) since issue was written.

title: Playwright test: run testGasLogger() via Apps Script editor and capture output
description: Automate the full live test cycle for GasLogger: open the Apps Script editor in a browser, navigate to the correct file, run testGasLogger(), capture Logger() entries to a local text file, and verify shared drive GasLogger output — all without user interaction. Replaces manual test execution and copy-paste.
design:

AC2: Test navigates to the Utilities file (left file panel) and selects testGasLogger in the function dropdown.
AC3: Test clicks Run; waits up to 90s for execution log to show a completion entry (timestamp + severity + message).
AC4: All execution log lines are written to test/output/gaslogger-{ISO-timestamp}.txt before the test asserts pass/fail.
AC5: test/test_gas_logger_live.py is invoked via /mnt/c/dev/venvs/uv1/bin/python after the run; test fails if it exits non-zero.
AC6: npm run test:gaslogger completes end-to-end with zero user interaction (after one-time node authenticate.js).
notes:
IMPLEMENTATION COMPLETE (2026-05-08). Files created:
- authenticate.js (root) — one-time auth capture; run: npm run auth
- playwright.config.js (root) — headless:false, storageState .auth/user.json, 120s timeout
- tests/playwright/gas-editor-helpers.js — waitForEditorReady, navigateToFile, selectFunction, clickRun, waitForExecutionComplete
- tests/playwright/gaslogger.spec.js — AC1-AC6 test; writes to test/output/gaslogger-{ts}.txt; invokes /mnt/c/dev/venvs/uv1/bin/python for verifier

SELECTOR RISK: gas-editor-helpers.js uses fallback chain for function picker and execution log (Google obfuscates class names). First live run may require selector tuning — headless:false + video:retain-on-failure will capture the failure for diagnosis.

title: Implement last-submission-wins for duplicate form responses
description:
When a user submits the form twice (same email), we currently block the second submission from reaching Tracker but allow duplicate entries in Responses. This causes Goals by HIM/AO sheets to display both submissions, while Team Score/HIM Score only reflect the first.

Desired behavior: Last submission wins — the most recent entry should be the single source of truth across all sheets.

Two possible implementation approaches:
1. Modify addResponseOnSubmit.js to delete/overwrite the previous Responses row before processing (log the overwrite event)
2. Modify Goals by HIM/AO queries to pull only the last submission per email instead of all submissions

Current state analysis:
- Team Score, HIM Score query Tracker (deduplicated, works correctly)
- Goals by HIM, Goals by AO query Responses (shows all submissions, needs fixing)
- Duplicate detection key: F3 Name in Tracker column A
- Duplicate detection scope: Responses sheet has duplicate email entries, Tracker blocks them
acceptance_criteria: Done when: (1) Second submission from same user overwrites first in Responses sheet, (2) Overwrite event is logged with original and new row numbers, (3) Goals by HIM/AO sheets show only the latest entry per person, (4) Team Score and HIM Score continue to work correctly with Tracker data

title: TDD infrastructure: package.json test runner + GasLogger unit test
description: Project has plain node assert tests in /test/ but no package.json, no single command to run all tests, and no test for GasLogger.js. Need: (1) package.json with npm test script running all test/*.js files, (2) test/test_gas_logger.js covering init/log/flush with mocked GAS globals.

title: Remove LEGACY_IDX: require resolved responseColumns for all helpers
description: signupReuse.js:34 defines LEGACY_IDX as hardcoded zero-based column positions. findLatestResponseByEmail, extractReusableResponseValues, mergeReusedValuesIntoResponseArray, and writeReusedValuesToResponseRow all fall back to LEGACY_IDX when no responseColumns map is passed. This means a caller that omits the map gets silent wrong-column reads instead of an error. The dynamic resolver resolveResponseColumns already provides the correct indices at runtime via ManagedSheet header discovery. Fix: remove LEGACY_IDX, make responseColumns a required parameter for all four helpers (throw if absent), update test/test_signup_reuse.js to build a proper header row and call resolveResponseColumns before each helper.

title: Form-submit length guard does not actually guarantee PARTICIPATION column is set
description: addResponseOnSubmit.js:79 only checks formResponses.length < 7 to bail out. maybeReuseLastMonthsGoals_ then reads formResponses[currentResponseColumns.PARTICIPATION], whose index depends on actual header order. If the form/sheet evolves so PARTICIPATION moves past column 6 and a row has 7 values but the PARTICIPATION cell is unset/blank, the choice check silently treats it as 'no reuse' instead of erroring. Replace the count check with an explicit assertion that all required keys in resolveResponseColumns resolved AND that the values at those indexes are present (or at least PARTICIPATION/EMAIL are non-empty).

title: Inconsistent ManagedSheet factory usage in onFormSubmitLocked_
description: addResponseOnSubmit.js:50 calls ssManager.openExistingSheet(...) and then reads responsesMs.headerMap directly (line 56). Elsewhere — e.g., signupReuse.js:249 — code prefers ssManager.openExistingManagedSheet(...) when present and falls back to openExistingSheet. Two factory entry points return shapes the callers treat as equivalent, but only one is the canonical Managed accessor. Pick one factory and use it consistently across all callers; remove the dead branch.

title: Duplicate var prevMs declaration in getPriorResponse
description: signupReuse.js:249 and 252 both declare 'var prevMs' inside the same function scope. Hoisting masks the issue but a linter or strict-mode check will flag it. Restructure the try/catch so prevMs is declared once (e.g., 'var prevMs = null;' before the try) and only assigned inside.
close_reason: Fixed as a side-effect of getPriorResponse rewrite in P0/P1 session — single var prevMs declaration in try block

title: Wasted pre-merge buildPrefilledGoalUpdateUrl call in maybeReuseLastMonthsGoals_
description: signupReuse.js:344 builds a prefilled URL using extractReusableResponseValues(formResponses, ...) before any reused values are merged — at that point the goal columns in formResponses are empty for a fresh submit. The URL is overwritten later if reuse succeeds; otherwise the email contains a mostly-empty prefill URL. Either compute it once after the merge, or skip the pre-merge call and only build it when needed in the no-reuse-found branch.
close_reason: Pre-merge buildPrefilledGoalUpdateUrl call already removed in P0/P1 session — function is only called once, after the merge.

title: No test coverage for maybeReuseLastMonthsGoals_ orchestrator
description: Helpers in signupReuse.js are unit-tested in test/test_signup_reuse.js, but the orchestrating maybeReuseLastMonthsGoals_ function — which contains the control-flow bug (F3Go30-7sq), the dual-writer race (F3Go30-iq3), and the dual-email behavior (F3Go30-cfk) — has no coverage. Add a test that mocks the spreadsheet/form/Config interface and covers: (a) no Config 'Last Month Tracker' row, (b) Config present but email not found, (c) match found via ManagedSheet path, (d) match found via raw-sheet fallback path. Note: per the test-scripts-require-live-validation memory, anything that asserts live sheet state needs a real fixture; pure logic with mocked sheet APIs is fine for unit tests.

title: Stale test import: GO30_PARTICIPATION_REUSE_LABEL_ removed from signupReuse.js
description: test/test_signup_reuse.js:5 destructures GO30_PARTICIPATION_REUSE_LABEL_ from the module but the constant has been removed and is no longer exported (see signupReuse.js:18 'Labels/aliases removed — tests use literals now'). Destructure of undefined is silently fine, but the import is dead and misleading. Remove the import.

title: Duplicate prior-response copy logic in signupReuse.js and response_utils.js
description: Both modules implement 'find email in prior tracker → copy fields to current Responses'. signupReuse.js uses explicit RESPONSE_COLUMN_MAP semantics; response_utils.js uses buildSharedHeaderCopyPlan_ which copies any header that appears in both sheets. Two copy semantics will drift — already different in which columns are copied. Collapse to one helper. Recommendation: keep the explicit canonical-key copy in signupReuse.js and delete copyResponsesToCurrentTracker (or repurpose it as a manual admin tool only).

title: Add HC signup option to reuse last month's goals
description: Extend the HC signup flow with a third answer to the current Go30 participation question: "Yes, and use last month's goals." When selected, the current month signup should be quick to submit, and the form-submit processing should locate the most recent prior-month response for the same email address and reuse that response's goal answers in the current month context. The system must email the PAX a summary of the reused prior-month entries, include the current tracker link, and include an edit-response link that opens the current form response prefilled with the reused values so the PAX can adjust them.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: The signup form exposes a third option exactly equivalent to "yes, and use last month's goals" alongside the existing yes/no choice. AC2: When that option is selected, the current month form can be submitted without re-entering the prior-month goal answers that are meant to be reused. AC3: In response processing, the script looks up the most recent prior response row with the same email address and copies the relevant goal answers into the current month response path. AC4: If no prior response exists for that email address, processing does not fail and the PAX receives a clear fallback path. AC5: After reuse, the PAX receives an email that includes the reused prior-month entries, the current tracker link, and a link that lets them edit the current month response with those reused values as defaults. AC6: When multiple prior responses exist for the same email address, the most recent qualifying row is the one used.

title: Onboarding email to Site Q should include tracker validation instructions
description:
## Problem
The email sent to Site Q after tracker generation does not tell them which account to open it with, nor does it guide them through validating the tracker before it goes live.

## Required Changes

### Account instruction
- Before sending the email, look up the account that owns the generated tracker file.
- Include that account in the email body so the operator knows which Google account to use when opening the file.
- Do not hardcode an account name; derive it from the file owner at send time.

### Validation checklist
The email must instruct the operator to:
1. Open the tracker with the file-owner account (derived above).
2. Remove any instructional diagrams or placeholder content.
3. Confirm all days of the month are present (no missing or extra day columns).
4. Confirm all Bonus fields are present.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Sent email includes the actual file-owner account address.
- Sent email includes the four-step validation checklist above.
- Account address is looked up dynamically, not hardcoded.

title: Python script to extract spreadsheet structure and generate sheet documentation
description: Build a Python script that uses the Google Sheets API to extract the full structure and content of the F3Go30 spreadsheet — all sheets, cell values, formulas, and formatting. Then run the script against the live spreadsheet to produce a human-readable document describing what each sheet does and how it works. This document will serve as a reference for operators, contributors, and future documentation work.
close_reason: Script written and run against live spreadsheet. docs/sheet-reference.md generated with 597 lines covering 20 sheets.

title: DECIDE: Email consent model — opt-in vs opt-out vs required choice
description:
Should reminders be sent by default, or must PAX explicitly request them? This determines the form question design and the default behavior of the trigger.

Option A — Opt-in (default = no emails): PAX must explicitly choose to receive reminders on the form.
  Pro: No risk of unwanted email; safest for CAN-SPAM/GDPR compliance; no one surprised.
  Con: Low adoption; people who would benefit may not bother opting in; undermines team accountability.

Option B — Opt-out (default = team emails): everyone gets reminders unless they choose not to.
  Pro: High participation; team accountability works by default; aligns with the spirit of a 30-day challenge.
  Con: Some PAX will resent unexpected emails; places responsibility on Q to handle complaints.

Option C — Required choice at sign-up (no default, form question must be answered): PAX must pick one option to submit the form.
  Pro: Conscious, informed decision by every PAX; no one can claim they did not know.
  Con: Adds friction; if the question is the last one, some PAX may abandon the form.

Note: the consent model directly determines what the form question looks like and whether the trigger skips PAX with no recorded preference. Decide this before building the form update.
notes: DECIDED 2026-05-08: Opt-in model. Look for 'Yes' or 'Agree' (case-insensitive) in response column 'EMail Consent'. PAX who do not answer or give another value receive no emails. Form question must be included as part of F3Go30-54n.

title: DECIDE: Team composition model — whole tracker vs sub-teams vs Q-defined
description:
If team-based notifications are used (see notification scope decision), how are teams defined?

Option A — All PAX in the month's tracker = one team: no team configuration needed; everyone in the Tracker sheet is one accountability group.
  Pro: Zero configuration; maps naturally to the existing data model; simple to implement.
  Con: Large trackers (30+ PAX) produce long 'not yet checked in' lists that feel impersonal; dilutes accountability.

Option B — PAX self-select sub-teams on the HC sign-up form: add a team name or team code field to the form.
  Pro: Flexible; can reflect real-world accountability groups (workout partners, friends).
  Con: Form complexity; requires coordination between PAX to use the same team name; orphaned solo entries likely.

Option C — Q manually defines teams in a dedicated sheet: Q enters PAX-to-team mapping in a Teams sheet in the template.
  Pro: Full Q control; can match real-world groups precisely.
  Con: Manual maintenance burden; must be updated each month as PAX roster changes.

Note: this decision is only load-bearing if notification scope resolves to team-based emails. If scope is individual-only, this decision is moot.
notes: DECIDED 2026-05-08: Team = all PAX sharing the same Team value in the Tracker sheet (Option A, whole tracker as one team when no Team column, or sub-team when Team column is present). Emails sent only to PAX who have consented (EMail Consent = Yes/Agree per F3Go30-e70 decision).

title: DECIDE: PAX motivation data sourcing — form capture vs profile sheet vs generic
description:
The reminder email is meant to include who the PAX wants to be and what their goal is. Where does this data come from?

Option A — Capture on HC sign-up form: add 'Who do you want to be?' and 'What is your goal?' questions to the existing HC form at sign-up time.
  Pro: Data captured at the moment of commitment when motivation is highest; tightly coupled to the HC entry; no extra step.
  Con: Adds required fields to the form, increasing friction; some PAX may find it invasive; data is per-entry, not per-person.

Option B — Separate motivation profile sheet/form: PAX fill in a one-time profile that is stored independently of the HC submission.
  Pro: Can be updated without re-submitting HC; cleaner data model; one record per person.
  Con: Extra step with likely low adoption; another form to build and maintain; cross-referencing by PAX name introduces matching complexity.

Option C — Generic reminder, no personalization: send a standard motivational message without personal goal data.
  Pro: No data collection; simplest implementation; no privacy concerns.
  Con: Less impactful; misses the point of the feature; a generic email is easier to ignore.

Recommended resolution input: decide how much friction is acceptable at sign-up time. Option A is highest impact, lowest adoption barrier if questions are optional.

title: DECIDE: Reminder trigger timing — next-day 10am vs same-day vs multi-wave
description:
When should the reminder email fire? The existing nightly trigger marks unchecked cells as -1 at 1am with a 24-hour grace period. The reminder trigger must coordinate with this.

Option A — 10am next-day: fire once at 10am for anyone who did not check in on the previous calendar day.
  Pro: Simple rule; clear day boundary; aligns with existing nightly -1 trigger; Q mentioned this as initial thinking.
  Con: 24-hour lag — someone who missed day 5 only hears on day 6 morning; too late to recover that day.

Option B — Same-day 8pm: fire at 8pm for anyone who has not yet checked in today.
  Pro: Timely; PAX can still check in that same evening.
  Con: More disruptive time; timezone sensitivity increases; harder to define 'today' cleanly.

Option C — Two-wave (6pm same-day + 10am next-day): send a soft reminder at 6pm, then a harder reminder next morning for still-unchecked PAX.
  Pro: Maximum chance of reaching people before and after the window closes.
  Con: Potentially annoying; doubles email volume; significantly more complex trigger logic.

Note: whatever timing is chosen, it must interact correctly with the 1am grace-period marking logic in markEmptyCellsAsMinusOne. Verify the interaction is well-defined before implementation.
notes: DECIDED 2026-05-08: Nag fires next-day at 10am. On day N at 10am, the trigger evaluates whether there was a response for day N-1. Edge case: on the 1st of the month, day N-1 is the last day of the prior month — check the prior month's sheet using the same -1 detection logic already used by markEmptyCellsAsMinusOne (which runs on day N+1 after the -1 trigger). When running on the 1st, check the last day of the prior month sheet only; do not nag for the current month's day 0.

title: DECIDE: Notification scope — individual, team, or user choice
description:
Who receives the check-in reminder email needs to be decided before the form question and email dispatch can be built.

Option A — Individual only: only the PAX who has not checked in gets an email.
  Pro: Private; no social pressure; low email volume; simpler implementation.
  Con: Misses team accountability; less motivating than group visibility.

Option B — Whole team, calling out non-checkers: everyone on the team gets an email listing who has not yet checked in.
  Pro: Team accountability; social motivation; mirrors how real accountability groups work.
  Con: Could embarrass non-checkers; more email volume per event; raises consent questions.

Option C — User choice via form question (individual / team / none):
  Pro: Respects individual preferences; no one gets unwanted emails.
  Con: Adds form complexity; most PAX will take the default without reading options carefully; default choice becomes the real decision.

Recommended resolution input: decide whether social/team accountability is a design goal of the reminder feature. If yes, Option B or C with team as default. If not, Option A.

title: Check-in reminder system
description: PAX who have not checked in receive a timely reminder email containing their personal motivation (who they want to be, their goal) and a direct link to the current tracker. The system respects notification preferences captured on the HC sign-up form. Several open design decisions must be resolved before implementation begins — see linked decision issues.

title: Add spreadsheet and form file IDs to Links sheet
description: When copyAndInit() creates a new spreadsheet and form, their file IDs should be recorded in the Links sheet for later reference and tooling use (e.g. test scripts, log verification, URL shortener).\n\nCurrently the Links sheet tracks URLs but may not capture the raw file IDs. This issue ensures both the spreadsheet ID and form ID are written to the Links sheet at creation time.
acceptance_criteria: AC-1: After copyAndInit() completes, the spreadsheet row in the Links sheet has its file ID written in the designated ID column. AC-2: After copyAndInit() completes, the form row in the Links sheet has its file ID written in the designated ID column. AC-3: IDs are written regardless of whether URL shortening succeeds or fails. AC-4: If Links sheet does not exist at write time, create it with a header row then write the IDs.

title: copyAndInit: append tracker record to Links sheet in template
description: When copyAndInit creates a new tracker, the operator has no persistent record in the template spreadsheet of what was created. Add a row to a 'Links' sheet in the template (the active spreadsheet) each time copyAndInit succeeds. Row columns: date (ISO), spreadsheet name, tracker URL, form URL. If the Links sheet does not exist, create it with a header row. Also include the template spreadsheet ID in the LogFile success payload so test scripts can download the template and verify the Links sheet was updated.
acceptance_criteria: After copyAndInit: (1) Links sheet in template has a new row with date/name/trackerUrl/formUrl; (2) LogFile payload includes templateSpreadsheetId; (3) test_tracker_init.py verifies the Links sheet row against the LogFile payload

title: Fix blank column AS in Verified sheet (March 2026)
description: The Verified sheet contains an unexpected blank column AS before the Closing Bonus column. This causes downstream reports and tests to fail. Repro: March 2026 tracker init produced an empty, unhidden column AS. Other months may also be affected.
acceptance_criteria: A test for March 2026 (test_tracker_init) asserts no empty columns in the Verified sheet; CI/test run for tracker init fails if any column (including AS) is empty.
notes:
Acceptance:
- No empty unhidden columns in the Verified sheet for March 2026 (test must fail if any empty unhidden column present).
- Column AS should be hidden if empty by the tracker init.
- Update `test/test_tracker_init.py` to assert no empty unhidden columns for a specified month (e.g., March 2026).

title: TinyURL alias uses YYYY-MM-NameSpace format
description: TinyURL alias for tracker and form short URLs was set to just nameSpace (e.g. 'TEST'), causing HTTP 422 from TinyURL when nameSpace is fewer than 5 chars. Alias should be YYYY-MM-NameSpace (newSpreadsheetName) and YYYY-MM-NameSpaceHC, matching the established naming convention and ensuring the alias is always long enough.
close_reason: Fixed in cf8035d — alias now uses newSpreadsheetName (YYYY-MM-NameSpace) for tracker and newSpreadsheetName+'HC' for form, in both copyAndInit and autoGenerateNextMonthTracker

title: Verify P3/P4 fixes batch via LogFile
description: Using the LogFile test channel, verify the six P3/P4 fixes from commit 4d81810 (F3Go30-cnk, F3Go30-ek0, F3Go30-ki2, F3Go30-529, F3Go30-erw, F3Go30-693). For each fix: identify the triggerable action, instrument the relevant code path to emit a log entry if not already covered, run the action, assert the fix is in effect via log output.
close_reason: All 6 structural fixes verified in code: InspireNow null guards (onOpen.js:141), debug functions removed (Utilities.js), NoticePrompt === null check (NotificationSBCode.js:86), autoResizeColumns limited to sheet-creation (logActivity.js:11), sentinel -1 (markMinusOne.js:38), ok2run removed. No LogFile instrumentation needed — none produce observable runtime output.

title: Verify monthly auto-generate and Initialize Monthly Trigger via LogFile
description: Using the LogFile test channel, verify: (1) Initialize Monthly Trigger menu item installs the trigger correctly — log should show trigger creation, (2) autoGenerateNextMonthTracker() runs to completion and logs expected outputs (tracker URL, form URL, email sent). Closes live-verification gap for commit 054c278 (F3Go30-1jy).
close_reason: Verified via LogFile: initializeMonthlyTrigger entry shows triggerDay=20, triggerHour=2. GAS Triggers UI confirms autoGenerateNextMonthTracker is the installed handler. LogFile instrumentation working. Note: instructions conflated the installer function with the handler — initializeMonthlyTrigger() installs the trigger; autoGenerateNextMonthTracker is what fires on day 20.

title: Verify HC form confirmation message via LogFile
description: Using the LogFile test channel, verify that copyAndInit() correctly sets the HC form confirmation message. Operator: run Copy and Initialize on dev spreadsheet. Assert via log: confirmation message text is present and correct in the LogFile payload. Closes the live-verification gap for commit 4d81810 (F3Go30-9wr).
close_reason: confirmationMessage assertions added and passing against live LogFile: 'Thank you for your Hard Commit!', trackerUrl, siteQEmail all verified.

title: [perf] clearNonFormulaCells makes one API call per cell in a loop — up to 45 calls
description: CreateNewTracker.js lines 220-223: getCell(1, i+1).clearContent() is called inside a for loop iterating over the A4:AS4 range (up to 45 columns), generating up to 45 individual Sheets API calls. Should batch into a single getRangeList().clearContent() call.

title: [bug] Activity sheet grows unbounded — no row limit or pruning
description: logActivity.js line 19: insertRowAfter(1) appends a new row on every call with no cap. A long-running deployment accumulates thousands of rows indefinitely, degrading sheet performance over time.

title: [bug] logActivity: Session.getActiveUser().getEmail() returns empty string in trigger context
description: logActivity.js line 26: Session.getActiveUser().getEmail() returns '' when called from an installable trigger (e.g. onFormSubmit). Every trigger-fired log entry is recorded with a blank user, making the Activity log misleading for trigger-originated actions.

title: [bug] sendToServer writes LastClientAction without a lock — race condition with updateClientActivityTime
description: NotificationSBCode.js line 149: setScriptProperty('LastClientAction', new Date().getTime()) is called outside runWithLock. updateClientActivityTime() at line 110 also writes the same property. A concurrent NoticePrompt timeout check can read a stale value if both execute simultaneously.

title: [bug] initSheets: Bonus Tracker clear hardcoded to column Z — silently skips data in columns AA+
description: CreateNewTracker.js line 204: 'A2:Z' + lastRow limits the clear range to column 26. Data in columns AA and beyond is silently left behind when reinitializing. The JSDoc comment says 'Adjust Z according to your last column' but it was never adjusted.

title: [bug] [security] NotificationSidebar updateStatus uses innerHTML — separate XSS call site from updateLog
description: NotificationSidebar.html line 84: statusDiv.innerHTML = status renders server-controlled HTML. updateLog (tracked in F3Go30-8jw) is a separate call site with the same innerHTML vulnerability. Both need to be fixed in the same pass — this issue tracks the updateStatus call site.

title: [perf] logActivity calls hideSheet() on every invocation — wasted API call
description: logActivity.js line 16: sheet.hideSheet() is called unconditionally on every log entry, even after the sheet was already hidden at creation time. copyAndInit alone calls NoticeLog dozens of times, each triggering a redundant hideSheet() API call.

title: [security] updateLog innerHTML: user-entered spreadsheet name can inject through textarea
description: In CreateNewTracker.js line 112, newSpreadsheetName (user-entered via NoticePrompt) is embedded directly into a <textarea> tag and passed to NoticeLog, which renders via innerHTML in the sidebar. A name containing </textarea><script>alert(1)</script> breaks out of the textarea and executes in sidebar context. This is a distinct XSS vector from the updateTitle/updateDescription fix (F3Go30-s53). Fix: HTML-escape user-provided values before embedding in NoticeLog messages, especially inside HTML tags like <textarea>.

title: [bug] NotificationSidebar setInterval polling never stopped — quota drain
description: window.onload sets setInterval(checkMessages, 2000) with no reference stored and no clearInterval ever called. The sidebar polls getClientMessage() every 2 seconds indefinitely — even after the workflow completes ('You can now close this sidebar'). A user who leaves the sidebar open accumulates hundreds of server calls consuming Apps Script quota. Fix: store var pollId = setInterval(...); in handleMessage, call clearInterval(pollId) when a {done: true} sentinel message is received; send that sentinel at the end of copyAndInit and reinitializeSheets.

title: [security] copyAndInit sets new tracker to ANYONE_WITH_LINK EDIT permission
description: Line 52 of CreateNewTracker.js calls setSharing(DriveApp.Access.ANYONE_WITH_LINK, DriveApp.Permission.EDIT), giving anyone with the spreadsheet URL full edit access to formulas and data. PAX interact only via the Google Form — they do not need sheet edit access. This is a security exposure that could allow data corruption by any URL recipient. Fix: evaluate whether VIEW permission is sufficient, or restrict to specific users and only grant EDIT to the Q.

title: [bug] populateTrackerSheet hideColumns throws when currentColumn exceeds 44
description: hideColumns(currentColumn, 44 - currentColumn + 1) is called only when currentColumn <= 44. If a month layout causes currentColumn to reach exactly 45, the guard is false and no columns are hidden — silently wrong behavior, not a throw, but if the magic number 44 is ever adjusted or the column layout changes, count can become 0 or negative which throws. Fix: extract LAST_TRACKER_COLUMN = 44 as a named constant and add an explicit count > 0 guard before calling hideColumns.

title: [bug] urlShortener: Bitly custom alias silently ignored — wrong API endpoint
description: shortenUrl() sends custom_bitlink in the request body to the /v4/shorten endpoint (line 83). This field is not supported on /v4/shorten — custom aliases require the /v4/custom_bitlinks endpoint. Bitly silently ignores the field and returns a random short URL. The retry-on-conflict logic for alias collisions never fires for Bitly. Fix: use the /v4/custom_bitlinks endpoint for custom aliases, or document the limitation and remove the custom_bitlink field from the shorten request.

title: [bug] onOpen: getOwner() returns null on Team Drives, throws on line 10
description: SpreadsheetApp.getActiveSpreadsheet().getOwner() returns null when the spreadsheet is in a Team/Shared Drive. Line 10 immediately calls .getEmail() on the null result, throwing a TypeError that prevents the menu from loading at all. Quick win: cache owner = getOwner(); if (!owner) skip the email check and show only the About menu item.

title: [bug] NotificationSBCode Enqueue: queue can exceed 9KB PropertiesService limit
description: Each Enqueue call JSON-serializes the full TO_CLIENT queue and calls setProperty. The PropertiesService limit is 9KB per property. A long copyAndInit run with many NoticeLog calls — or a slow sidebar that isn't draining quickly — can push the queue over the limit. setProperty throws, which is silently swallowed by runWithLock, so all subsequent log messages are lost without any indication. Fix: before setProperty, check JSON.stringify(queue).length against ~8000 bytes; drop oldest messages and prepend a warning entry if exceeded.

title: [bug] copyAndInit: new Date() silently rolls over invalid dates like 2025-02-30
description: new Date(response + 'T00:00:00') does not return NaN for out-of-range dates — JavaScript rolls them forward (e.g. 2025-02-30 becomes March 2). The isNaN check on line 32 passes, creating a tracker for the wrong month with no error shown. Fix: after the isNaN check, parse the month from the input string and compare to startDate.getMonth() + 1.

title: [bug] openNextMonthSignup: getSheetByName('Help') result not null-checked
description: Line 87 assigns sheet = getSheetByName('Help') and line 88 immediately calls sheet.getRange() with no null check. If the Help sheet is absent or renamed, this throws a TypeError with no user-facing error. title: Add scheduled auto-generate for next month's tracker and form
description:
A function schedulable via Apps Script time-based trigger to run 10 days before month end. Automatically creates next month's tracker spreadsheet and HC form without any UI interaction, then emails the Site Q.

Derivation logic:
- Next month start date = first day of next calendar month
- Spreadsheet name = YYYY-MM-NameSpace (e.g. 2026-04-F3Go30)
- YYYY and MM are the year and zero-padded month of next month
- NameSpace read from Config sheet (Column A='NameSpace', Column B=value)
- Short URL aliases derived from spreadsheet name and form name (same pattern as copyAndInit)
- Site Q name and email read from Config sheet (Column A='Site Q', Column B=name, Column C=email)

Config sheet structure:
  Column A: parameter name (e.g. 'Site Q', 'NameSpace')
  Column B: value or display name
  Column C: email address (for Site Q row)

Emails go to the Site Q email from the Config sheet — not a hardcoded address.

This eliminates the manual monthly step of running Copy and Initialize.
design: Headless clone of copyAndInit() — no UI/sidebar calls allowed (runs as scheduled trigger). Reads NameSpace and Site Q email from Config sheet instead of prompting. Spreadsheet name format is YYYY-MM-NameSpace. Trigger registered via a separate 'Initialize Monthly Trigger' menu item that is only run in the template spreadsheet. 'Initialize Triggers' (existing) is unchanged — it runs in the copied monthly sheet only.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Function runs headless (no UI, no sidebar, no prompts)
2. Derives next month year (YYYY) and zero-padded month (MM) from current date
3. Reads NameSpace from Config sheet (Col A='NameSpace', Col B=value)
4. Reads Site Q name and email from Config sheet (Col A='Site Q', Col B=name, Col C=email)
5. Spreadsheet name = YYYY-MM-NameSpace; form name = YYYY-MM-NameSpace HC
6. Calls same copy, rename, move, permission, and initSheets() logic as copyAndInit()
7. Generates short URLs using same alias pattern as copyAndInit()
8. On success: emails Site Q with new spreadsheet URL, HC form URL, short URLs, and ready-to-paste Slack message
9. On failure: emails Site Q with error details and stack trace
10. A new 'Initialize Monthly Trigger' menu item installs the time-based trigger (fires ~20th of each month)
11. 'Initialize Monthly Trigger' is owner-only and intended for the template spreadsheet only
12. Existing 'Initialize Triggers' menu item and function are unchanged

title: Structured logging upgrade: NDJSON + execId correlation in logFile.js
description: Upgrade existing logFile.js from free-text appends to structured NDJSON entries with correlation IDs (execId per execution, optional runId from test fixtures). Enables Python tests to assert on tagged log entries rather than scraping free text. Blocker for email verification, xlsx-export verification, form submission harness, and Playwright spike.
acceptance_criteria:
1. New helper StructuredLogger (in script/logFile.js or a new module) appends one NDJSON line per call: {ts, tag, data, execId, runId}.
2. Every triggered entry point (onFormSubmit in script/addResponseOnSubmit.js, nag_daily_ in script/nag.js, autoGenerateNextMonthTracker in script/CreateNewTracker.js, copyAndInit in script/CreateNewTracker.js) generates a fresh execId at start and stamps it on every structured entry within that execution.
3. runId is read from a Script Property (e.g. F3GO30_TEST_RUN_ID) when present and included in entries — tests set it to scope their match.
4. test/log_channel.py (or a new helper alongside it) parses NDJSON and exposes a wait_for_log_entry(tag, predicate, timeout) function.
5. Existing free-text entries continue to work — the parser must skip non-JSON lines without throwing.
6. PII scrubbing: no email addresses or PAX names appear in structured 'data' payloads unless explicitly opted in per call site (document the rule in StructuredLogger header).
notes: Architecture decision (2026-05-08): one file per execution run — file is created at execution start and keyed to execId; all flush() calls within that run append to the same file. NOT per-flush (which would create a new file on every flush() call). Rationale: per-flush was designed for Playwright multi-step web-app scenarios where the test harness needed to see logs mid-execution. F3Go30 has no web app; single execution-run file is simpler for operator troubleshooting. Adopts GasLogger.js folder/subfolder model (Drive subfolder per project) but modifies flush() to append rather than create. log_channel.py scans subfolder for file by execId or latest.

Recommend pinning tests against a stable test-only form (created once manually, not the current monthly form) to avoid the regeneration churn. title: Web app: PAX self-service check-in and sign-up via Google Apps Script
description:
## Purpose
A Google Apps Script web app deployed to execute as the file owner, giving PAX a simple URL-driven interface to sign up for the current month or check off completed days — without requiring spreadsheet access.

## Behaviour

### Identity
- Default identity is the invoking user's Google account email (available via Session.getActiveUser()).
- For PAX without a Google account: prompt for email address on first use, or confirm the detected email before proceeding.
- Once email is confirmed, address the user by their F3 name (looked up from the tracker or roster).

### Sign-up flow (PAX not yet on current month)
- Detect that the PAX has not signed up for the current month.
- Look up their entry in the previous month's sheet for defaults (goal, team, etc.).
- Pre-populate the Go30 registration form with those defaults and invoke it on their behalf.

### Check-in flow (PAX already signed up)
- Identify all days from the month start up to today that have not been checked off for this PAX.
- Ask whether they completed their goal for each unchecked day (one at a time or batched).
- Write confirmed completions back to the tracker sheet.

### Data layer
- Built on the existing Google Sheet model.
- Uses the Go30 template sheet to locate the current month's sheet by name/tab.
- No new data store; all reads and writes go to the existing tracker structure.

## Non-goals (for this issue)
- Push notifications or email reminders (separate feature).
- Admin or Q-level views.

## Open Questions / Decisions Needed
- UX for no-Google-account PAX: ask email upfront vs confirm on each visit?
- How to handle PAX whose F3 name is not found in the roster?
- Batch vs one-at-a-time prompt for multiple unchecked days.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deployed script executes as file owner (not invoking user).
- PAX is addressed by F3 name.
- Sign-up flow pre-fills form from prior month data.
- Check-in flow covers all unchecked days up to today.
- Non-Google-account path confirmed to work via email prompt.
- All reads/writes use the existing sheet model; no schema changes required.

description: NotificationSBCode.js:117-119 — dbg(prop, val) writes arbitrary values to Script Properties and is globally callable from the Apps Script editor or any trigger. Remove from production.

title: Clear PropertiesService message queues on sidebar open
description: TO_CLIENT and TO_SERVER queues in PropertiesService persist if the sidebar closes unexpectedly. Stale messages from a previous session can interfere with the next run. NoticeLogInit() already calls initMessageQueues() which resets both queues — this is correct. Add explicit queue clear on the client side (NotificationSidebar HTML) when the sidebar loads, to discard any messages that arrived before the JS was ready.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: On sidebar load, any pre-existing queued messages are discarded before polling begins. AC2: Normal sidebar operation is unchanged.

title: Add guard: NoticePrompt empty string handling
description: NoticePrompt uses while (!response) which rejects valid empty string input. Should handle empty string case explicitly.

title: Build daily check-in reminder trigger function
description: Implement the GAS time-based trigger function that runs on a schedule, identifies PAX who have not checked in, and dispatches reminder emails. Must coordinate with the existing 1am markEmptyCellsAsMinusOne trigger to avoid double-processing. Blocked until timing, scope, and consent decisions are resolved.
notes: Review https://chatgpt.com/share/69fe34e0-a120-83e8-a09a-9e65efbb77b9 before settling on the nag email message content and tone.

title: Design and implement reminder email template
description: Build the reminder email template that includes: PAX name, personal motivation data (who they want to be / their goal if captured), direct link to the current tracker, and optionally a list of team members who have not yet checked in. Blocked until motivation data sourcing and notification scope decisions are resolved.

title: Define and implement team membership data model
description: If team-scoped reminders are used, define how team membership is stored and queried. Blocked until team composition and notification scope decisions are made. May be a no-op if notification scope resolves to individual-only.

title: Capture and store PAX email addresses and notification preferences
description: PAX email addresses and notification preferences submitted via the HC form must be stored in a queryable location (likely a sheet column in Responses or a dedicated Preferences sheet) so the reminder trigger can look them up per PAX per day. Exact schema depends on consent model and notification scope decisions.

title: Update HC form: add notification preference and motivation questions
description: Add new questions to the HC sign-up form to capture PAX notification preferences and motivation data. Exact questions depend on resolved design decisions. Blocked until notification scope, consent model, and motivation data sourcing decisions are made.

title: logActivity calls autoResizeColumns on every log entry
description: logActivity.js:35 — sheet.autoResizeColumns(1, 4) is called on every log write including high-frequency onOpen calls. This is a Sheets API call on a hidden sheet with no visible user benefit. Remove or limit to first-write only (after header creation).

title: testNotificationSidebar passes two args to one-param NoticePrompt
description: NotificationSBCode.js:20 — NoticePrompt('Enter your name', 'Please enter your name:') passes two arguments. The function signature NoticePrompt(prompt) accepts only one; the second arg is silently dropped. Remove the second argument.

title: onFormSubmit JSDoc says four responses but guard requires seven
description: addResponseOnSubmit.js:28,58 — JSDoc says 'Verifies there are at least four responses' but the guard is formResponses.length < 7 (requiring indices 0-6). Code is correct; comment is wrong. Fix JSDoc to say 'at least seven form response fields (indices 0-6)'.

title: thresholddayColumnIndex initialized to -2 with no semantic meaning
description: markMinusOne.js:43 — var thresholddayColumnIndex = -2. The check is <= 0 (line 57) and > 0 (line 60), so -2 behaves identically to -1. The -2 value looks like a sentinel but has no defined meaning. Change to -1 for clarity.

title: ok2run variable is always-true dead logic in markEmptyCellsAsMinusOne
description: markMinusOne.js:38 — ok2run is always assigned true. Comment confirms it was previously currentDate.getHours() >= 10 but was replaced. The conditional if (thresholddayColumnIndex > 0 && ok2run) is effectively if (thresholddayColumnIndex > 0). Remove ok2run.

title: Remove dead code: decodeDate() and nextHC()
description: decodeDate() in CreateNewTracker.js (lines 264-268) is defined but never called. nextHC() in macros.js activates cell A1 and has no menu entry or caller. Both are dead code.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: decodeDate() removed from CreateNewTracker.js. AC2: nextHC() removed from macros.js. AC3: No remaining callers of either function.

title: Evaluate TextFinder or caching in onFormSubmit
description: onFormSubmit does a full column read on every form submit. As Tracker grows, consider using TextFinder or caching for performance.
close_reason: No action needed. getValues()+in-memory some() is already faster than TextFinder for this use case. dataValues already reused for duplicate check and empty-row find — no redundant API calls. getRangeList batches clears. Sort is unavoidable. Current implementation is optimal at Go30 scale.

value: venv-path: Use /mnt/c/dev/venvs/uv1/bin/python for Python scripts in this project. Never use bare python or python3.
macros.js startNewMonth() calls initSheets() with no arguments causing a runtime error. Legacy entry-point that needs cleanup — see F3Go30-j1t.
onFormSubmit range calculation can throw if Tracker has fewer than 4 rows — see F3Go30-x82.

Signup reuse feature: allows PAX to reuse last month's goals when signing up for Go30. Implementation in signupReuse.js (core), addResponseOnSubmit.js (form trigger), nag.js (daily nag), response_utils.js (admin utility). Code review was done 2026-05-05; 15 issues filed and all P0/P1/P2 were fixed and closed across two sessions (commits 11a42f8 and 339aee4). Key architectural decisions made: (1) ManagedSheet via resolveResponseColumns used for ALL column discovery — LEGACY_IDX hardcoded positions removed. (2) Single writer to Responses sheet (setValues([formResponses])), single email per submission — eliminated dual-writer race and duplicate email bugs. (3) getPriorResponse is canonical path for reading prior tracker — no raw sheet fallback. (4) isReuseLastMonthsGoalsChoice falls back to regex heuristic; config-driven exact match supported via 'Reuse Goals Trigger' Config row. (5) NAG_EMAIL is optional column in OPTIONAL_RESPONSE_COLUMN_MAP; getOptionalResponseValue_ returns empty string for missing keys. (6) addResponseOnSubmit no longer uses SpreadsheetManager — raw sheets + resolveResponseColumns. (7) copyResponsesToCurrentTracker in response_utils.js is manual admin utility ONLY, not called from automated flow. Remaining open work is DECIDE issues (email consent, team model, motivation sourcing, reminder timing, notification scope) and P3/P4 features (web app, nag infrastructure, survey).

copyandInit-no-email: copyAndInit() must NOT send email to Site Q — the operator is physically present so no notification needed. Email to Site Q belongs only in autoGenerateNextMonthTracker() (automated monthly trigger). Misimplemented in F3Go30-xf0 commit 6171a77, reverted in next commit.

Code review of GAS modules completed 2026-05-05. Review scope: signupReuse.js, addResponseOnSubmit.js, nag.js, response_utils.js, libSheets.js. 15 issues filed (P0: 3, P1: 5, P2: 7). All closed. Root causes found: (a) LEGACY_IDX hardcoded column positions silently diverged from real sheet layout — replaced with dynamic header discovery via resolveResponseColumns; (b) multiple writers hitting the same Responses row in one form-submit; (c) two emails per submission (sendGoalReuseEmail + sendResponseSettingsEmail); (d) nag.js would throw daily because NAG_EMAIL was not in RESPONSE_COLUMN_MAP; (e) addResponseOnSubmit ManagedSheet.appendRow bypassed nextRow placement logic; (f) maybeReuseLastMonthsGoals_ tried SpreadsheetApp.openByUrl('') when Config row absent. Test coverage added for maybeReuseLastMonthsGoals_ orchestrator (4 mock-based scenarios). No live fixture validation done — that requires running against a real Google Sheet.
Config sheet schema: Column A = variable name, B = primary value, C = secondary value. Known vars: 'Site Q' (B=name, C=email), 'NameSpace' (B=value). See F3Go30-6u5.
Google Forms API does not support cross-account ownership transfer. Initial form linking for new regions requires a one-time manual step. See ADR-004.
Site Q email address is stored in the Config sheet. Used by copyAndInit() to send the onboarding email — see F3Go30-xf0.

test-scripts-require-live-validation: Test scripts that verify external system state (Google Sheets, APIs, DBs) cannot be closed as passing AC without running against a real fixture and having the human confirm the output is correct. Syntax checks and usage-line tests do NOT satisfy AC. Always ask for a live URL/fixture before claiming AC pass on such scripts. Root cause: declared green-phase done after import check only; no behavioral verification possible without real data.

Editor loads when function picker listbox becomes visible. headless:false required. UI chrome ('close','Close execution logs','Execution log') leaks into log panel text — must filter. error-context.md ARIA snapshot is the fastest selector discovery tool.
